lesson.subtitle: Aula 10 -- Making Suggestions -- Should, Could, Would
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rafael de Andrade Brandão | Aula 10 -- Making Suggestions

listenings.text: Hi, this is Diana from Costa Logistics. Thank you for the product pitch last week. The new solution looks faster and more reliable, and I am very interested. But I have a small problem: my budget is limited, and I am not sure which plan is the best one for my team. There are too many options, and I need your honest advice. Please tell me what you recommend. Should we start with the basic plan, or should we choose the pro plan right away? Could we improve the old system first and upgrade later? I would really like a clear proposal with one strong recommendation. Please consider my budget and call me with your best suggestion. Thank you, and talk to you soon.

Hi, this is Rafael. I just prepared my proposal for Diana. Her budget is limited, so I will give her one clear recommendation. I think she should start with the pro plan, because it is more reliable and it saves more time. We could add a small upgrade later, but I would not recommend the basic plan for her team. In my proposal, I will suggest two options and explain why the pro plan is the better choice. I will also propose a simple way to improve her old system during the change. My advice is honest and clear, and I am sure she will consider it carefully. I will call her tomorrow with the final suggestion. Talk to you soon.

extra_audio.text: Rafael reads Diana's message and prepares a proposal. He considers her limited budget and her main goal. He suggests two options: the basic plan and the pro plan. He recommends the pro plan because it is more reliable. Finally, he proposes a small upgrade for later and asks for her decision.
